data.title: Redeye: Thunderful Group - One step in the right direction
data.description:
Redeye provides an update on Thunderful Group following its Q1 2025 report, which was better than expected. With the latest restructuring program now starting to yield its full effect, the organization has been streamlined, and it is now time for revenues to deliver in order for the company to reach profitability. As such, the upcoming quarters are becoming increasingly important, and the company’s financial position leaves limited room for error.

Read more and download the Research Update.

Follow companies at Redeye to receive the latest equity research within Life Science and Technology.

This is a press release from Redeye - Research Powered Investment Banking. www.redeye.se/

data.title: Dubai National Insurance & Reinsurance Resumes Trading on Dubai Bourse
data.description:
Dubai National Insurance & Reinsurance (DFM:DNIR) recommenced trading on the Dubai Financial Market on Friday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started Thursday and was lifted after the insurance company published the results of its board meeting.

data.title: Al Ain Alahlia Insurance Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Al Ain Alahlia Insurance (ADX:ALAIN) restarted trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Friday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started on Thursday and was lifted after the Abu Dhabi-based insurance and reinsurance services company published the results of its board of directors meeting.

data.title: Eutelsat Signals Openness to Equity Issue — Market Talk
data.description: Eutelsat's executives didn't rule an equity issue as they expect funding from export credit agencies won't be enough to cover its financing plan, Bryan Garnier's Antoine Lebourgeois says in a research note. The remarks came after the French satellite company reported third-quarter revenue that was slightly lower on year, but in line with expectations, the analyst says. "Management indicated ongoing work on a revised financing plan, with [export credit agencies] expected to cover most, though not all, of funding needs, and signalled openness to equity issuance," the analyst says. The company said in February it was working on a financing plan in line with its strategy and long-term objective for debt reduction. Shares fall 6.7% to 3.68 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Beijing Fourth Paradigm Technology's Q1 Profit Rises 30%
data.description:
Beijing Fourth Paradigm Technology recorded a total revenue of around 1.08 billion yuan in the first quarter of 2025, up 30% from a year prior, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The improved revenue came in the backdrop of a boost in AI development and transformation in China, according to the software company.

data.title: European Stocks on Track to Log Fifth Straight Week of Gains
data.description:
The STOXX 50 edged up 0.2% and the STOXX 600 gained 0.6% on Friday, with both indexes on track to close the week approximately 1.8% higher, potentially marking a fifth consecutive weekly advance.

Investors continued to weigh the prospects of a de-escalation in the ongoing trade war.

On Friday, healthcare, telecommunications, and oil & gas sectors led the gains, while technology stocks lagged behind.

Meanwhile, traders digested a fresh round of corporate earnings.

Shares of Richemont surged around 4% after the luxury goods group reported a better-than-expected 7% increase in Q1 revenues.

Swiss Re also posted strong results, with a 16% rise in quarterly profit.

data.title: Bigger-than-expected Q1 earnings loss drags Tokmanni shares
data.description:
** Shares in Tokmanni slump around 12% after the Finnish discount retailer posted bigger-than-expected Q1 core earnings loss

** Its quarterly adjusted EBIT loss of 11.2 million euros($12.56 million) was bigger than the 4.7 million euro loss consensus cited by Inderes

** Inderes analyst Arttu Heikura thinks the weak Q1 will lead to a downward revision of forecasts after the Q1 report

** Tokmanni points to negative sales impact from the timing of Easter and a mild winter in the Nordics, adding that, in Finland, sales were weakened by strikes

** If losses hold, Tokmanni share tracks its worst day since July 2024

($1 = 0.8918 euros)

data.title: Axactor ASA: Employee share option plan 2025
data.description:
In accordance with the authorization granted by the AGM on 6 May 2025, theboardof directors of Axactor ASA has decided to establish a long-term incentiveprogram (LTI 2025) for senior executives employed by the Axactor group. Theplanencompasses up to 3 500 000 shares. The company's liability with respect tothescheme will be met through holdings of treasury shares or by issuing newshares.

Under the LTI 2025, participants will receive share options, which, if certainpredefined performance criteria are met within a performance period, can beexercised by paying the predefined strike price. The strike price is set astheaverage share price of the last 30 days prior to grant. One share option givesacontingent entitlement to one share after paying the strike price.

The following criteria determine the award of options during the three-yearperformance period 1/3: employed when the performance period ends1/3: share price development equal to or better than peers1/3: return on equity meeting target set by the board of directors

Vesting of share options presumes that the individual is an employee of theAxactor group. The total gain from exercised options during one calendar yearshall not exceed 200 per cent of the participant's base salary at grant. Thenumber of options will be adjusted in the event of a dividend payout topreserve, but not increase, the value of the share option allocation.

The LTI 2025 is effective from 6 May 2025 and the share options will be vestedafter three years.

Primary insiders in the company have been allocated and accepted the followinggrants of options according to the terms described above

- Johnny Tsolis, CEO, has been granted 950 000 share options. Following thegrant, he holds 4 266 667 options in the company. - Nina Mortensen, CFO, has been granted 175 000 share options. Following thegrant, she holds 858 333 options in the company. - Kyrre Svae, Chief of Strategy and IR, has been granted 275 000 shareoptions.Following the grant, he holds 1 183 333 options in the company. - Arnt Andre Dullum, COO, has been granted 175 000 share options. Followingthegrant, he holds 983 333 options in the company. - Karl Mamelund, Chief of Investments, has been granted 275 000 share options.Following the grant, he holds 800 000 options in the company. - Vibeke Ly, Chief of Staff, has been granted 175 000 share options. Followingthe grant, she holds 983 333 options in the company.

Further the following have been granted and accepted share options accordingtothe terms described above

- Stina Koren, country manager 3PC in Norway, has been granted 150 000 shareoptions. Following the grant, she holds 1 008 333 options in the company.

- Lisa Sohtell, country manager NPL in Sweden and Norway, has been granted 100000 share options. Following the grant, she holds 908 333 options in thecompany.

- Heidi Piispanen, country manager in Finland, has been granted 100 000 shareoptions. Following the grant, she holds 783 333 options in the company.

- Yvonne Wagner, country manager in Germany, has been granted 100 000 shareoptions. Following the grant, he holds 100 000 options in the company.

- Antonio Cataneo, country manager in Italy, has been granted 125 000 shareoptions. Following the grant, he holds 1 058 333 options in the company.

- Andres Lopez, country manager in Spain, has been granted 400 000 shareoptions. Following the grant, he holds 2 033 333 options in the company.

Of the 3 500 000 share options, 3 000 000 share options have been granted.

data.title: Redeye: EG7 - Solid growth in Q1 and continued potential ahead
data.description:
Redeye updates on EG7 after Q1-results where sales came in above expectations while EBITDA was somewhat soft. While we trim estimates, we continue to see topline and earnings growth potential in coming quarters, supported by new game releases (Palia and the Cold Iron game project) coupled with cost savings and M&A optionality.

data.title: 17LIVE Group Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
17LIVE Group repurchased 1,600 shares for SG$1,451 on Thursday, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The social entertainment platform has been authorized to buy back around 18.2 million shares. To date, it has bought back 321,500 shares.

data.title: Market Chatter: CBA's Valuation Remains 'Unjustifiable,' Morningstar Says
data.description:
Commonwealth Bank of Australia's valuation is "unjustifiable," as the bank's shares continue to climb despite concerns over its high price, according to a Friday report by The Australian, citing Morningstar Equity Market.

Morningstar's analyst Lochlan Halloway said that while CBA's latest earnings met expectations, the premium investors are willing to pay for its earnings is excessive.

data.title: American Axle Seeks Secondary Listing in UK as Dowlais Merger Progresses
data.description:
New York-listed American Axle and Manufacturing Holdings intends to seek a secondary listing on the London bourse's main market as part of its merger with Dowlais Group , according to a Friday release.

The automotive components company's cash and share combination with UK-listed automotive-focused specialist engineering group Dowlais was agreed in January and remains on track for regulatory approvals.

The secondary listing is not a condition for the completion of the merger, the company noted.

data.title: Asian currencies rally as weak US data raises prospect of more Fed cuts
data.description:
Key points:
  • Rupiah leads Asian FX rally as US dollar weakens
  • Poor US data boosts Fed rate cut expectations
  • Bank Indonesia expected to hold interest rate in Wednesday call

The Indonesian rupiah led a broader advance across Asian currencies on Friday as the U.S. dollar retreated, with Treasury yields tumbling after lacklustre U.S. economic data reaffirmed expectations for more Federal Reserve policy easing.

The rupiah strengthened as much as 0.7% to 16,395 per dollar, staging a notable recovery despite being the region's worst performer year-to-date with losses exceeding 2%.

"The rupiah is playing catch up to the recovery of other Asian currencies, after an extended bout of underperformance, riding also on better sentiments in the onshore stock market," said Radhika Rao, senior economist at DBS Bank.

With inflation still within the target range, policymakers have room to reinforce their pro-growth stance with an interest rate cut this month, Rao said.

Bank Indonesia will deliver its policy decision on Wednesday, and ING analysts anticipate rates will remain unchanged.

Other regional currencies also advanced, with the Malaysian ringgit and Philippine peso each gaining 0.3%, while the Thai baht and Singapore dollar both added 0.2%.

The South Korean won rose 0.4%, advancing for a fourth consecutive session in its third straight week of gains.

The currency broke through the psychologically important 1,400 level on Wednesday with a 1.8% surge following talks between South Korea and the U.S. on May 5.

The sharp movement mirrors the Taiwanese dollar's early-May rally, when it surged 6% over two days. On Friday, it edged up 0.2%, heading for a seventh consecutive weekly gain.

This week had kicked off optimistically after the U.S.-China trade truce, which initially boosted the dollar, but momentum quickly faded, leaving most currencies trading sideways.

By 0614 GMT, the U.S. dollar index was flat at 100.56 points, though it remained up 0.3% for the week.

The greenback struggled to recover after Thursday's slide following unexpected U.S. producer price declines in April, which followed earlier benign consumer price data, strengthening expectations for at least two Fed rate cuts this year.

The MSCI emerging market currency index (.MIEM00000CUS) held steady on Friday but was poised for its fifth consecutive weekly gain, up over 0.3%.

India's rupee bucked the regional trend, dipping 0.1% after earlier gains.

Bankers attributed the weakness to persistent dollar demand for immediate payments from a large state-run bank and two major foreign banks, despite positive sentiment from the India-Pakistan truce.

Regional equity markets cooled in the final days of the week after earlier gains on easing trade tensions.

Jakarta and Bangkok shares each rose over 0.3%, while Seoul and Taipei TWSE:TAIEX added 0.3%. Manila stocks fell 0.9% and Singapore declined 0.2%.

The MSCI emerging Asian equities gauge (.MIMS00000PUS) eased 0.3% on Friday but has gained over 3% this week, on track for its fifth straight weekly advance.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Taiwan meets with US for tariff talks in South Korea

** Malaysia's economy grows 4.4% in first quarter

** Malaysia's current account surplus expands

data.title: 99 Speed Mart Retail's Profit, Revenue Rise in Q1
data.description:
99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings' profit attributable to owners rose to 143.2 million ringgit in the first quarter from 133.2 million ringgit a year earlier.

Earnings per share went up to 0.017 ringgit from 0.0159 ringgit a year ago, according to a Thursday filing with Bursa Malaysia.

Revenue climbed to 2.61 billion ringgit from 2.43 billion ringgit a year ago, the mini-market retailer said.

data.title: Europe's STOXX 600 set for fifth straight weekly rise
data.description:
European shares extended gains on Friday, supported by healthcare stocks, and were on track to end the week in positive territory as benign headlines about a U.S.-China temporary truce sparked risk-on sentiment.

The region-wide STOXX 600 index rose 0.4% by 0710 GMT, set for its fifth consecutive weekly advance. Other local bourses also traded in the green at open, with Germany's hovering near a record high.

The heavyweight healthcare sub-index (.SXDP) led gains with a 1.4% rise, boosted by Novo Nordisk and Novartis .

The region was closely monitoring developments from the Russia-Ukraine peace talks, a day after Russian President Vladimir Putin declined to meet face-to-face with Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskiy.

According to Vladimir Medinsky, the head of the Russian delegation, talks between the representatives of the two warring countries are scheduled for the day.

Investors awaited euro zone trade balance figures for March as well as Italy’s inflation rate, due later in the day.

Among individual stocks, reinsurance company Swiss Re fell 1.2%. The company posted claims of $570 million from deadly wildfires in Los Angeles earlier this year.

Luxury group Richemont reported a slightly stronger-than-forecast 7% rise in quarterly sales, taking its shares 4% higher in early trading.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: NAB Former Fraud Executive Sues Bank for Over AU$1.5 Million
data.description:
(Updates to add National Australia Bank's response to MT Newswires' request for comment in the fourth paragraph)

National Australia Bank is being sued by its former fraud operations chief, Kate Prendergast, for more than AU$1.5 million, alleging claims of unfair dismissal and anti-competitive conduct, according to a Friday report by the Australian, citing a Federal Court hearing.

Prendergast filed a fair work claim eight months ago but now seeks to expand the case by including claims under the Competition and Consumer Act, per the report.

The Bank's barrister confirmed that both parties are open to discussing changes to the claim ahead of the next court date in July, the report added.

In response to MT Newswires' comment request, a spokesperson declined to comment further as the matter is currently before the Court.

data.title: Redeye: Navigo Q1 2025 - Solid prospects despite softer quarter
data.description:
Redeye updates its estimates and valuation following Navigo releasing its Q1 2025 report. While Q1 figures missed our projections somewhat, we judge well-filled deal pipelines and order books should result in profitable growth for subsidiaries during the rest of 2025e. We update our estimates and valuation.

Read more and download the Research Update.

Follow companies at Redeye to receive the latest equity research within Life Science and Technology.

This is a press release from Redeye - Research Powered Investment Banking. www.redeye.se/

data.title: Kennedy-Wilson Holdings Insider Bought Shares Worth $1,272,000, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
William J McMorrow, Director, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, on May 15, 2025, executed a purchase for 200,000 shares in Kennedy-Wilson Holdings for $1,272,000. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, McMorrow has control over a total of 11,972,373 common shares of the company, with 3,823,562 shares held directly and 8,148,811 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1408100/000140810025000122/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1747351251.xml

data.title: Nine Entertainment Poised for 'Significant' Net Cash Position After Domain Deal, Jarden Says
data.description:
Nine Entertainment Co. Holdings will be in a strong net cash position following the completion of the Domain Holdings Australia deed with CoStar Group and a dividend payment, according to a Thursday report by the Jarden research.

As Domain's majority shareholder, Nine had earlier said it intends to vote in favor of the proposed scheme, where Nasdaq-listed real estate firm CoStar will acquire all the remaining shares in Domain for AU$4.43 per share in cash.

Post the deal, Nine said it expects to pay a fully franked special dividend of AU$0.47 to AU$0.49 per share to its shareholders.

On a pro forma basis, Jarden expects Nine Entertainment's net cash to range between AU$120 million and AU$170 million after the merger.

In addition, Jarden expects the company to pay a special dividend of AU$0.48 per share, which shows that the market is currently valuing the remainder of NEC, including pro forma net cash, at AU$1.05 per share.

The firm believes Nine also has the potential capacity for a merger & acquisition, or capital management.

Jarden has an overweight rating on both Nine Entertainment and Domain Holdings, with price targets of AU$1.80 and AU$4.43, respectively.

data.title: Global Indemnity Group Insider Bought Shares Worth $1,011,752, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Saul A Fox, 10% Owner, Director, on May 13, 2025, executed a purchase for 33,800 shares in Global Indemnity Group for $1,011,752. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Fox has control over a total of 2,662,400 Class A common shares of the company, with 2,662,400 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1494904/000095017025073057/xslF345X05/ownership.xml

data.title: IMCD to Buy Spanish Food Additives Distributor Tecom Ingredients
data.description:
IMCD agreed to purchase Spain-based distributor of food ingredients and additives, Tecom Ingredients.

The deal is anticipated to be finalized in the second quarter, pending customary closing conditions, the Dutch specialty chemicals and ingredients company said Friday.
